content: Hey, I just got back from a trip to Costa Rica last week. The birdwatching there was incredible!
That sounds amazing! What kinds of birds did you see?
I spotted a resplendent quetzal - it's this brilliant green bird with a long tail. Took me three days of hiking to finally see one.
Wow, three days of hiking sounds intense! Was it worth it?
Absolutely. I'm Taylor, by the way. I'm an agricultural scientist working on drought-resistant crops back in California.
Nice to meet you, Taylor! How do you like working on drought-resistant crops?
It's rewarding but challenging. My back has been killing me lately from sitting at the lab bench too long. I'm thinking about trying acupuncture.
I'm sorry to hear about your back pain. Acupuncture can be really helpful for some people. Have you tried any other treatments?
Just physical therapy so far. Not much improvement. On a happier note, I've gotten really into speedcubing recently - can solve a 3x3 in about 45 seconds now.
That's impressive! How long have you been practicing?
About six months. I find it's a good way to give my eyes a break from staring at soil samples all day. I also paint landscapes in my free time.
Do you paint from photos or go out to paint on location?
Mostly from photos since my back doesn't love standing for long periods. I did a painting of those cloud forests in Costa Rica though - the mist was so ethereal.
That must have been beautiful. Do you have a favorite bird from your trip?
Besides the quetzal, I loved seeing the scarlet macaws. Their colors are unreal in person. I'm already planning my next birdwatching trip to Ecuador in December.
Ecuador sounds like a great destination! Are you planning to visit the Galápagos Islands?
Not this time - focusing on the Amazon basin and cloud forests. I need to save vacation days for when my research team goes to Chile next summer for a conference.
Sounds like you have some exciting travels ahead! Best of luck with your back treatment and your research work.
